text: ASK the caregiver about the child's problems, then LOOK for signs of illness.
source_quote: ASK the caregiver about the child's problems, then LOOK for signs of illness.

text: What are the child's problems?
source_quote: 3.1 ASK: What are the child's problems?

text: Does the child have a cough? If yes, for how long? A cough for 14 days or more is a danger sign.
source_quote: Ask: Does the child have a cough? If yes, for how long? A cough for 14 days or more is a danger sign

text: Does the child have fever now or in the last 3 days? If yes, when did it start? Fever for 7 days or more is a danger sign.
source_quote: Ask: Does the child have fever now or in the last 3 days? If yes, when did it start? Fever for 7 day

text: For how long? Is there blood in the stool? Diarrhoea for 14 days or more is a danger sign. Blood in the stool is a danger sign.
source_quote: Ask: For how long? Is there blood in the stool? Diarrhoea for 14 days or more is a danger sign. Bloo

text: Ask the caregiver to offer the child a drink or breastfeed.
source_quote: Ask the caregiver to offer the child a drink or breastfeed.

text: Try to wake the child by clapping or speaking loudly.
source_quote: Try to wake the child by clapping or speaking loudly.

text: A convulsion during the current illness is a danger sign. The child needs urgent referral.
source_quote: A convulsion during the current illness is a danger sign. The child needs urgent referral.

text: Count the child's breaths for one full minute while the child is calm and quiet.
source_quote: Count the child's breaths for one full minute while the child is calm and quiet.

text: Look at the lower chest wall when the child breathes IN. Chest indrawing is when the lower chest wall goes in when the child breathes in.
source_quote: Look at the lower chest wall when the child breathes IN. Chest indrawing is when the lower chest wal

text: Use the MUAC (Mid-Upper Arm Circumference) strap on children age 6 months up to 5 years. Wrap the strap around the child's left upper arm.
source_quote: Use the MUAC (Mid-Upper Arm Circumference) strap on children age 6 months up to 5 years. Wrap the st

text: Also check for swelling of both feet (oedema). Press thumbs gently on the top of each foot for 3 seconds.
source_quote: Also check for swelling of both feet (oedema). Press thumbs gently on the top of each foot for 3 sec

text: YELLOW: moderate malnutrition (refer to supplementary feeding if available).
source_quote: YELLOW: moderate malnutrition (refer to supplementary feeding if available)

text: RED: severe malnutrition - DANGER SIGN - refer urgently.
source_quote: RED: severe malnutrition - DANGER SIGN - refer urgently

text: Do a Rapid Diagnostic Test (RDT) for malaria before treating. Do not give antimalarial medicine unless the RDT is positive.
source_quote: Do a Rapid Diagnostic Test (RDT) for malaria before treating. Do not give antimalarial medicine unle

text: 1. Check expiry date; do not use expired tests. 2. Wear new gloves for each child. 3. Clean the child's finger with an alcohol swab.
source_quote: 1. Check expiry date; do not use expired tests. 2. Wear new gloves for each child. 3. Clean the chil

text: If RDT is negative: do not give antimalarial. Follow up in 3 days. If fever persists at follow-up, refer.
source_quote: If RDT is negative: do not give antimalarial. Follow up in 3 days. If fever persists at follow-up, r

text: 1. Wash hands with soap and water. 2. Pour entire ORS packet into a clean container. 3. Add 1 litre of clean water. 4. Stir until dissolved.
source_quote: 1. Wash hands with soap and water. 2. Pour entire ORS packet into a clean container. 3. Add 1 litre

text: Dissolve the zinc tablet (or half tablet) in breast milk or clean water on a spoon. Give the full 10-day course.
source_quote: Dissolve the zinc tablet (or half tablet) in breast milk or clean water on a spoon. Give the full 10

text: Check expiration date. Crush the tablet and mix with breast milk or water. Give the first dose in front of the caregiver.
source_quote: Check expiration date. Crush the tablet and mix with breast milk or water. Give the first dose in fr

text: Give first dose immediately. Second dose 8 hours later. Then morning and evening for 2 more days. Crush tablets and mix with breast milk or water.
source_quote: Give first dose immediately. Second dose 8 hours later. Then morning and evening for 2 more days. Cr

text: If the child spits out the entire dose, give another.
source_quote: If the child spits out the entire dose, give another.

text: If unable to take medicine, refer to health facility.
source_quote: If unable to take medicine, refer to health facility.

text: Give more fluids and continue feeding.
source_quote: Give more fluids and continue feeding.

text: Return immediately if the child: cannot drink or feed, becomes sicker, or has blood in stool.
source_quote: Return immediately if the child: cannot drink or feed, becomes sicker, or has blood in stool.

text: Sleep under an insecticide-treated bednet (ITN).
source_quote: Sleep under an insecticide-treated bednet (ITN).

text: Keep all medicine out of reach of children. Store medicine in a clean, dry place.
source_quote: Keep all medicine out of reach of children. Store medicine in a clean, dry place.

text: Explain to the caregiver why the child must go to the health facility.
source_quote: Explain to the caregiver why the child must go to the health facility.

text: Write a referral note including: child's name, age, date, reason for referral, danger signs found, treatments given.
source_quote: Write a referral note including: child's name, age, date, reason for referral, danger signs found, t

text: Give the first dose of any appropriate medicine before referral.
source_quote: Give the first dose of any appropriate medicine before referral.

text: Help arrange transportation.
source_quote: Help arrange transportation.

text: If fever, keep the child cool during travel.
source_quote: If fever, keep the child cool during travel.

text: If diarrhoea, give ORS solution during travel.
source_quote: If diarrhoea, give ORS solution during travel.

text: Follow up the child after return, at least once a week until well.
source_quote: Follow up the child after return, at least once a week until well.

text: Follow up ALL children treated at home in 3 days. If the child is not better or is worse at follow-up, refer.
source_quote: Follow up ALL children treated at home in 3 days. If the child is not better or is worse at follow-u

text: After 3 days: Is the child still having diarrhoea? If yes and for less than 14 days, continue ORS and zinc. If 14 days or more, refer.
source_quote: After 3 days: Is the child still having diarrhoea? If yes and for less than 14 days, continue ORS an

text: After 3 days: Does the child still have fever? If RDT was positive and antimalarial was given, the child should be improving. If still febrile, refer.
source_quote: After 3 days: Does the child still have fever? If RDT was positive and antimalarial was given, the c

text: After 3 days: Is the child still coughing? Count breaths again. If still fast breathing, refer. If breathing is normal, the child is improving.
source_quote: After 3 days: Is the child still coughing? Count breaths again. If still fast breathing, refer. If b

text: For every visit, the CHW completes the Sick Child Recording Form. Record: date, CHW name, child's name, age, sex, caregiver name, address.
source_quote: For every visit, the CHW completes the Sick Child Recording Form. Record: date, CHW name, child's na
